section.para: If the argument that users in these scenarios are misclassified workers (employees or independent contractors) were successful, the implications, as you suggested, would be significant.

It would suggest that the foundational contracts of the digital economy—the Terms of Service—are potentially contracts of adhesion that misclassify the true nature of the relationship to extract valuable labor and IP without fair compensation.

listitem.para: Back Wages and Benefits: For labor provided (e.g., by Local Guides, dedicated VRP researchers, or structured beta testers) that should have been compensated under fair labor standards.
Compensation for IP: Claims for the fair market value of the intellectual property (vulnerability reports, creative content, training data) transferred to Google under arguably unconscionable terms.
Systemic Restructuring: Changes to how platforms engage with and compensate the individuals whose contributions are integral to their operation and success.
